channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This watchdog is tracking how AI firms are quietly backing off their safety pledges
item.description: The companies racing to own the AI future have built their technologies and businesses by carefully keeping track of you and your data.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Snow, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok is facing a rare meme drought. Inside the Great Meme Depression of 2025
item.description: People with a healthy limit on their screen time probably haven’t noticed—but there’s been a meme shortage this March. On TikTok, some have declared a full-blown “Meme Drought,” dubbing it the “Great Meme Depression of 2025.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: 23andMe says ‘delays’ resolved after users rushed to delete data
item.description: 23andMe Inc. said Tuesday that it experienced website delays, after a wave of customers sought to delete their genetic data before it can be sold through a planned bankruptcy auction.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Mapfre apuntala su estrategia de IA sobre un manifiesto rector y un nuevo centro global
item.description: Toda una declaración de intenciones. Mapfre presenta en sociedad su nuevo Manifiesto por una inteligencia artificial (IA) humanista, ética y responsable, una hoja de ruta que define una visión y misión claras en torno a una de las tecnologías más disruptivas de los últimos tiempos.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: SAP adoption surges in Europe as enterprises embrace cloud
item.description: Enterprises in Germany, Austria, and Switzerland are accelerating their transition to cloud-based ERP solutions, with SAP playing a key role in their digital transformation strategies.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The Toyota FT-Me: A Revolutionary Quadricycle for Urban Mobility
item.description: Discover the innovative features of the Toyota FT-Me, a compact electric quadricycle designed for urban mobility, sustainability, and accessibility.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cómo hacer frente al cambio cultural de las metodologías ágiles
item.description: Un escaso cuarto de siglo ha pasado desde que un grupo de 17 programadores se reuniese en Utah para debatir sobre viejos y nuevos métodos de desarrollo de software.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Warning for developers, web admins: update Next.js to prevent exploit
item.description: Developers and web admins using the Next.js framework for building or managing interactive web applications should install a security update to plug a critical vulnerability.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Striking the balance between information sharing and security
item.description: That balancing act requires a complex response, especially when designing networks and systems for collaboration. The post Striking the balance between information sharing and security first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Napster sold to tech commerce company for $207 million
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A brand that was notoriously connected to music piracy before reemerging as a subscription music service has been sold to Infinite Reality for $207 million.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Hollow Knight: Silksong gets new Steam metadata changes, convincing everyone and their mother that the game is finally releasing this year
item.description: New changes to Hollow Knight: Silksong's Steam metadata suggests that the long-awaited game will arrive this year.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Waymo plans DC robotaxi service debut as US plans new standards
item.description: Waymo, the robotaxi business of Google parent Alphabet Inc., plans to launch service in Washington, DC, next year, a move that would effectively give US lawmakers and other officials more exposure to self-driving cars as they mull a framework for federal standards.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: These genetically engineered houseplants are 30 times better at clearing toxins
item.description: It’s not every day that microbial genetics leads to a chic influencer party in Los Angeles. Yet there stood Patrick Torbey, the lone scientist in a plant-filled wine bar, addressing a roomful of stylish guests nibbling artisan crackers topped with melted Brie.
item.dc:creator: Susan Karlin, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia launches AgentIQ toolkit to connect disparate AI agents
item.description: As enterprises look to adopt agents and agentic AI to boost the efficiency of their applications, Nvidia this week introduced a new open-source software library — AgentIQ toolkit — to help developers connect disparate agents and agent frameworks.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cybersecurity Frameworks Fall Short in Preventing Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: A research paper finds that current cybersecurity frameworks are missing essential components to prevent supply chain attacks, highlighting the need for a more comprehensive approach.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Top House Dem seeks details on FOIA staffing amid agency firings
item.description: Rep. Gerry Connolly (D-Va.) is seeking data on FOIA staffing and compliance across federal agencies, which have long struggled with FOIA staff shortages. The post Top House Dem seeks details on FOIA staffing amid agency firings first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: NHL Launches Front Office App for General Managers and Executives
item.description: The National Hockey League (NHL) has introduced a front office app that streamlines roster, contract, and salary cap information for general managers and league executives.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: From first to smart, how the cloud now underpins every federal mission
item.description: OMB’s Cloud First and Cloud Smart policies put a stake in the ground for how agencies can change the way they deliver services to citizens. The post From first to smart, how the cloud now underpins every federal mission first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network
